profiles.label: Anxiety disorder
profiles.notes: Naturally elevated resting HR, lower HRV

profiles.label: COPD patient
profiles.notes: Chronically low SpO2, should not always alarm

profiles.label: Athlete w/ low resting HR
profiles.notes: Very low resting HR, very high HRV — exercise jumps are large

scenarios.name: resting_calm
scenarios.description: User at rest, all vitals at baseline

scenarios.name: resting_slight_hr_up
scenarios.description: Mild HR elevation within normal range (stood up, drank coffee)

scenarios.name: sleeping
scenarios.description: User sleeping — low HR, high HRV, stable SpO2

scenarios.name: light_walking
scenarios.description: Casual walking — slight HR bump, motion detected

scenarios.name: deep_breathing
scenarios.description: Relaxation exercise — HRV transiently high

scenarios.name: post_meal
scenarios.description: After eating — slightly elevated HR, temp

scenarios.name: ambient_temp_shift
scenarios.description: Moved to warmer environment — skin temp rises naturally

scenarios.name: moderate_exercise
scenarios.description: Jogging / cycling — HR elevated, high motion, SpO2 normal

scenarios.name: vigorous_exercise
scenarios.description: Sprinting / HIIT — HR very high, strong motion

scenarios.name: exercise_cooldown
scenarios.description: Post-exercise cooldown — HR gradually dropping, still moving slightly

scenarios.name: exercise_warmup
scenarios.description: Starting exercise — HR climbing, motion increasing

scenarios.name: stress_hr_mild
scenarios.description: Mild psychological stress — HR elevated, other signals normal

scenarios.name: stress_hr_moderate
scenarios.description: Moderate stress — HR clearly above baseline at rest

scenarios.name: stress_hr_absolute
scenarios.description: HR above absolute 120bpm threshold — clear stress at rest

scenarios.name: stress_spo2_mild
scenarios.description: SpO2 dip below 94% — clinical concern, single signal

scenarios.name: stress_hrv_suppressed
scenarios.description: HRV suppression from chronic stress — HR normal, HRV very low

scenarios.name: stress_panic_onset
scenarios.description: Panic attack onset — sudden HR spike at rest, HRV drops

scenarios.name: distress_hr_spo2
scenarios.description: HR elevated + SpO2 dropping — two primary signals

scenarios.name: distress_hr_hrv
scenarios.description: HR elevated + HRV suppressed — autonomic distress

scenarios.name: distress_spo2_hrv
scenarios.description: SpO2 dropping + HRV suppressed — respiratory distress pattern

scenarios.name: distress_triple_signal
scenarios.description: HR + SpO2 + HRV all deviated — severe physiological distress

scenarios.name: distress_with_fever
scenarios.description: HR elevated + temperature elevated + HRV suppressed — possible infection

scenarios.name: emergency_spo2_critical
scenarios.description: SpO2 < 90% — medical emergency, immediate escalation

scenarios.name: emergency_hr_extreme
scenarios.description: HR > 150 at rest — tachycardia emergency

scenarios.name: emergency_combined
scenarios.description: HR > 150 + SpO2 < 90% — multiple emergency signals

scenarios.name: edge_finger_off
scenarios.description: Ring removed / finger not detected — frame rejected

scenarios.name: edge_charging
scenarios.description: Device on charger — frame rejected

scenarios.name: edge_low_sqi
scenarios.description: Sensor noise — accepted but SQI very low

scenarios.name: edge_transient_hr_spike
scenarios.description: One-frame HR spike (motion artifact) — should NOT trigger stress

scenarios.name: edge_temp_only_elevated
scenarios.description: Temperature elevated alone — weak signal, should stay normal

scenarios.name: edge_exercise_plus_spo2
scenarios.description: Exercising + SpO2 drops — should NOT be suppressed

scenarios.name: edge_copd_baseline
scenarios.description: COPD patient with chronically low SpO2 — should not alarm at their baseline

scenarios.name: edge_anxiety_resting
scenarios.description: Anxiety patient at rest — higher HR is their normal

scenarios.name: edge_recovery_after_distress
scenarios.description: Returning to normal after a distress episode — recovery phase

scenarios.name: edge_cold_start
scenarios.description: Baseline not yet established — only absolute thresholds active

scenarios.name: transition_normal_to_stress
scenarios.description: Gradual stress onset — HR rising over multiple frames

scenarios.name: transition_stress_to_distress
scenarios.description: Escalating from stress to distress — SpO2 starts dropping too

scenarios.name: transition_distress_to_normal
scenarios.description: Distress resolving — vitals returning to baseline over time
